lit: They repaid me evil things instead of good things
sm: They repaid me evil for good,

syn.mc: Main clause of the scriptural quotation, continued in lines 552-555.
syn.svo: Subject: implied hostile plural. Verb: Ἀνταπέδωκάν. Indirect object: μοι. Direct object: κακὰ. Prepositional phrase: ἀντὶ ἀγαθῶν.
syn.rhet: Sharp antithesis of κακὰ / ἀγαθῶν opens the accusation.

lit: And childlessness to my soul,
sm: and bereavement to my soul,

syn.mc: Coordinate extension of the direct object begun in line 551.
syn.svo: The governing verb remains Ἀνταπέδωκαν from line 551. Added object: ἀτεκνίαν. Dative phrase: τῇ ψυχῇ μου.
syn.rhet: The complaint intensifies from moral evil to personal desolation.

tok: aor mid ptcp nom pl m
aor act ptcp nom pl m
lit: Having devised evil things against me, having said
sm: as they plotted evil against me and said,

syn.mc: Participial frame modifying the implied subject of Ἀνταπέδωκαν.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the quotation follows in lines 554-555.
syn.svo: Participles: λογισάμενοι, εἰπόντες. Object of λογισάμενοι: κακὰ. Prepositional phrase: ἐπ᾽ ἐμέ.
syn.ptc: The aorist participles describe the hostile intent and speech accompanying the repayment of evil.
syn.rhet: Participial accumulation heightens deliberation before the quoted plot.

lit: Let us bind the righteous one
sm: Let us bind the righteous man,

syn.mc: Deliberative/hortatory quotation dependent on εἰπόντες in line 553.
syn.sub: The causal ὅτι-clause follows in line 555.
syn.svo: Verb: Δήσωμεν. Object: τὸν δίκαιον.
syn.rhet: The direct quotation makes the persecutors' resolve stark and immediate.

lit: Because he is troublesome to us?
sm: because he is troublesome to us.

syn.mc: Causal clause explaining the resolve of line 554.
syn.sub: ὅτι introduces the reason attributed to the speakers.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὁ δίκαιος. Predicate adjective: δύσχρηστος. Dative of reference: ἡμῖν. Verb: ἐστιν.
syn.rhet: The persecutors justify violence by casting righteousness as inconvenience.

src: τί ἐποίησας, ὦ ᾿Ισραήλ, τὸ καινὸν ἀδίκημα;
ana: τί ἐποίησας, ὦ Ἰσραήλ, τὸ καινὸν ἀδίκημα;
lit: What new wrongdoing did you do, O Israel?
sm: What new outrage have you committed, O Israel?

syn.mc: Direct rhetorical question.
syn.svo: Interrogative object: τί, further specified by τὸ καινὸν ἀδίκημα. Verb: ἐποίησας. Vocative: ὦ Ἰσραήλ.
syn.app: τὸ καινὸν ἀδίκημα stands in apposition to τί, specifying the deed asked about.
syn.rhet: Apostrophe to Israel launches the accusatory section.

tok: aor act ptcp acc sg m
lit: You dishonored the one having honored you,
sm: You dishonored the one who honored you,

syn.mc: Main clause in the anaphoric accusation against Israel.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle is attributive.
syn.svo: Verb: ἠτίμησας. Object: τὸν τιμήσαντά σε.
syn.ptc: τιμήσαντά is attributive to the implicit referent of the object: 'the one who honored you'.
syn.rhet: First member of a tightly parallel series of accusation clauses.

tok: aor act ptcp acc sg m
lit: You glorified the one having glorified you,
sm: You gave glory to the one who glorified you,

syn.mc: Second parallel accusation clause.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle is attributive.
syn.svo: Verb: ἠδόξησας. Object: τὸν δοξήσαντά σε.
syn.ptc: δοξήσαντά is attributive within the object phrase.
syn.rhet: Verbal repetition and reversal continue the accusatory parallelism.

tok: aor act ptcp acc sg m
lit: You denied the one having acknowledged you,
sm: You denied the one who had acknowledged you,

syn.mc: Third parallel accusation clause.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀπηρνήσω. Object: τὸν ὁμολογήσαντά σε.
syn.ptc: ὁμολογήσαντά is attributive to the person Israel has denied.
syn.rhet: The sequence escalates from dishonor to outright denial.

tok: aor act ptcp acc sg m
lit: You renounced the one having proclaimed you,
sm: You renounced the one who proclaimed you,

syn.mc: Fourth parallel accusation clause.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀπεκήρυξας. Object: τὸν κηρύξαντά σε.
syn.ptc: κηρύξαντά is attributive in the object phrase.
syn.rhet: The anaphoric cadence carries the indictment forward.

tok: aor act ptcp acc sg m
lit: You killed the one having made you alive.
sm: You killed the one who gave you life.

syn.mc: Fifth and climactic accusation clause.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀπέκτεινας. Object: τὸν ζωοποιήσαντά σε.
syn.ptc: ζωοποιήσαντά is attributive and identifies the slain benefactor.
syn.rhet: The sequence culminates in a paradoxical reversal: killing the giver of life.

src: τί ἐποίησας, ὦ ᾿Ισραήλ; ἢ οὐ γέγραπταί σοι"
ana: τί ἐποίησας, ὦ Ἰσραήλ; ἢ οὐ γέγραπταί σοι·
lit: What did you do, O Israel? Or has it not been written to you:
sm: What have you done, O Israel? Or has it not been written for you:

syn.mc: Rhetorical question followed by a second question introducing scripture.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the citation itself begins in lines 565-566.
syn.svo: First clause: object τί, verb ἐποίησας, vocative ὦ Ἰσραήλ. Second clause: impersonal verb γέγραπται with dative σοι.
syn.rhet: The accusation is reinforced by appeal to written law.

lit: You shall not shed innocent blood,
sm: You shall not shed innocent blood,

syn.mc: Direct legal citation.
syn.sub: The purpose clause follows in line 566.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐκχεεῖς, negated by οὐκ. Object: αἷμα ἀθῷον.
syn.rhet: The law is quoted in concise prohibitory form.

lit: So that you may not die badly?
sm: so that you may not come to a miserable end.

syn.mc: Purpose clause dependent on the prohibition of line 565.
syn.sub: ἵνα introduces purpose or preventative result.
syn.svo: Verb: θάνῃς, negated by μή. Adverb: κακῶς.
syn.rhet: The law grounds the prohibition in consequences for the transgressor.

ana: Ἐγὼ μέν, φησὶν Ἰσραήλ, ἀπέκτεινα τὸν κύριον.
lit: I indeed, says Israel, killed the Lord.
sm: "I did kill the Lord," says Israel.

syn.mc: Direct self-incriminating statement with parenthetical speaker tag.
syn.svo: Subject: Ἐγώ. Verb: ἀπέκτεινα. Object: τὸν κύριον. Parenthesis: φησὶν Ἰσραήλ.
syn.app: Ἰσραήλ identifies the speaker parenthetically after φησίν.
syn.rhet: Prosopopoeia gives Israel a voice only to condemn itself.

src: διὰ τί; ὅτι ἔδει αὐτὸν ἀποθανεῖν.
lit: Why? Because it was necessary for him to die.
sm: Why? Because, it says, he had to die.

syn.mc: Short direct question followed by a causal answer.
syn.svo: Question phrase: διὰ τί. In the answer, impersonal ἔδει governs the infinitive ἀποθανεῖν with accusative subject αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: The terse objection-answer format prepares the rebuttal.

tok: pres mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: You have gone astray, O Israel, devising such things
sm: You have gone astray, O Israel, by contriving such arguments

syn.mc: Main clause of rebuke.
syn.svo: Verb: πεπλάνησαι. Vocative: ὦ Ἰσραήλ. Object of σοφιζόμενος: τοιαῦτα.
syn.ptc: σοφιζόμενος is circumstantial, describing the manner of Israel's error.
syn.rhet: The rebuke answers Israel's self-justification with ironic language of cleverness.

lit: Over the slaughter of the Lord.
sm: about the slaughter of the Lord.

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase completing the rebuke of line 569.
syn.svo: The phrase ἐπὶ τῇ τοῦ κυρίου σφαγῇ depends on σοφιζόμενος.
syn.rhet: The whole rationalization is exposed as sophistry about the Lord's slaughter.

src: ἔδει αὐτὸν παθεῖν,; ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ"
ana: ἔδει αὐτὸν παθεῖν, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ·
lit: It was necessary for him to suffer, but not by you;
sm: He did have to suffer, but not at your hands;

syn.mc: Concessive main assertion beginning a repeated rebuttal pattern.
syn.svo: Impersonal ἔδει governs the infinitive παθεῖν with accusative subject αὐτόν. Adversative phrase: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ.
syn.rhet: First member of the anaphoric concession-and-rebuttal series.

src: ἔδει αὐτὸν ἀτιμασθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ"
ana: ἔδει αὐτὸν ἀτιμασθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ·
lit: It was necessary for him to be dishonored, but not by you;
sm: He had to be dishonored, but not by you;

syn.mc: Second member of the concession-and-rebuttal series.
syn.svo: Impersonal ἔδει with infinitive ἀτιμασθῆναι and accusative subject αὐτόν. Adversative phrase: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ.
syn.rhet: Parallel repetition intensifies the denunciation.

src: ἔδει αὐτὸν κριθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ"
ana: ἔδει αὐτὸν κριθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ·
lit: It was necessary for him to be judged, but not by you;
sm: He had to be judged, but not by you;

syn.mc: Third member of the concession-and-rebuttal series.
syn.svo: Impersonal ἔδει with infinitive κριθῆναι and accusative subject αὐτόν. Adversative phrase: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ.
syn.rhet: The repeated syntax produces judicial hammering.

src: ἔδει αὐτὸν κρεμασθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ σοῦ
ana: ἔδει αὐτὸν κρεμασθῆναι, ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ τῆς δεξιᾶς
lit: It was necessary for him to be hanged, but not by the right hand
sm: He had to be hanged, but not by the right hand
emd: Adopted the user-supplied corrected reading across lines 574-575: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ τῆς δεξιᾶς σου.

syn.mc: Fourth member of the concession-and-rebuttal series, completed by line 575.
syn.svo: Impersonal ἔδει with infinitive κρεμασθῆναι and accusative subject αὐτόν. Adversative agent phrase begins here: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ τῆς δεξιᾶς ...
syn.rhet: The climax of the repeated series is sharpened by the concrete image of the guilty hand.

lit: of you.
sm: your.
emd: Adopted the user-supplied corrected reading across lines 574-575: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὑπὸ τῆς δεξιᾶς σου.

syn.mc: Possessive genitive completing δεξιᾶς from line 574.
syn.svo: No finite verb; σου depends on δεξιᾶς in the preceding line.
syn.rhet: The delayed possessive completes the image of Israel's own hand as agent.

src: ταύτην, ὦ ᾿Ισραήλ, πρὸς τὸν θεὸν ὥφειλες βοῆσαι τὴν φωνήν"
ana: ταύτην, ὦ Ἰσραήλ, πρὸς τὸν θεὸν ὥφειλες βοῆσαι τὴν φωνήν·
lit: This cry, O Israel, you ought to have cried out to God:
sm: This is the cry you ought to have raised to God, O Israel:

syn.mc: Main clause introducing the proper response Israel should have spoken.
syn.sub: The quoted prayer follows in lines 577-583.
syn.svo: Object/fronted phrase: ταύτην ... τὴν φωνήν. Verb: ὥφειλες. Complementary infinitive: βοῆσαι. Goal phrase: πρὸς τὸν θεόν. Vocative: ὦ Ἰσραήλ.
syn.app: ταύτην and τὴν φωνήν form a split object by hyperbaton.
syn.rhet: The speech turns from accusation to a counterfactual prayer.

src: ἔσποτα, εἶ καὶ ἔδει σου τὸν υἱὸν παθεῖν
ana: Δέσποτα, εἰ καὶ ἔδει σου τὸν υἱὸν παθεῖν
lit: Master, even if it was necessary for your son to suffer
sm: Master, even if it was necessary for your Son to suffer,
emd: Followed change_log.txt for ἔσποτα -> Δέσποτα. Also normalized εἶ -> εἰ as an obvious accent/OCR error required by the conditional syntax.

syn.mc: Conditional protasis beginning the quoted prayer.
syn.sub: εἰ καί introduces a concessive conditional frame continued in line 578.
syn.svo: Vocative: Δέσποτα. Impersonal ἔδει governs παθεῖν with accusative subject τὸν υἱόν; σου modifies υἱόν.
syn.rhet: The prayer concedes divine necessity before denying Israel's role in it.

lit: And this your will,
sm: and if this was your will,

syn.mc: Nominal continuation of the protasis begun in line 577.
syn.sub: No new finite verb; an implied ἦν/ἐστίν is understood in the concessive structure.
syn.svo: Subject/predicate nominal phrase: τοῦτό ... τὸ θέλημα, with σου as possessive genitive.
syn.rhet: The clause restates divine purpose in balanced parallel with the necessity statement.

src: πασχέτω δή, ἀλλὰ ὑπ᾽ ἐμοῦ μή"
ana: πασχέτω δή, ἀλλὰ ὑπ᾽ ἐμοῦ μή·
lit: Let him suffer then, but not by me;
sm: Then let him suffer, but not at my hands;

syn.mc: Apodosis of the conditional prayer.
syn.sub: Dependent on the concessive condition of lines 577-578.
syn.svo: Verb: πασχέτω. Adversative prohibition of agency: ἀλλὰ ὑπ᾽ ἐμοῦ μή.
syn.rhet: The prayer grants the event while disavowing participation in it.

lit: Let him suffer by foreigners,
sm: Let him suffer at the hands of foreigners,

syn.mc: First alternative imperative expanding the apodosis of line 579.
syn.sub: The imperative sequence continues in lines 581-583.
syn.svo: Verb: πασχέτω. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ ἀλλοφύλων.
syn.rhet: The alternatives transfer guilt away from Israel to outsiders.
